inline_comments.message:
I think you need replaceAll here because the key could occur more than once in the string.

We propbably should put in a test for that case as well.

It's OK for a key in the execution properties not to be in the URL so this exception should not be thrown.

However, if a key is specified in the URL, it must be in the execution properties so we need to check for that.
Maybe check the value of rcctp.getUrl() here as well, it should be http://some.place.that.does.not/exist, I think.
Yes, we need to pass the properties to the producer side.
Same as the comments on the RestClient.
Understand, will change the logic of this part
OK, will add check in unit test.

Thanks! Also maybe in the error message if a key is missing on the URL it would be really good if the error message mentioned the name of the key that was missing, something like:

key "myKey" specified on url "http://somewhere.in.the.{myKey}/{yourkey}" not found in execution properties passed by the current policy.

Maybe, improve the url syntax checking, for eg. nested tags will cause faulty final urls here.
Also handle missing tag value in properties.

We actually need to loop over the keys found in the URL so if we wrote a getKeysFromUrl() method on the Properties class, that could return the keys over which we iterate.

Set<String> urlKeys = restProducerProperties.getKeysFromUrl();

Thinking about this again, there is a possibility for interference here. We are overwriting the value of the URL parameter every time in every thread and this will cause problems if multiple instances are using this plugin. For example, if there are ongoing calls for multiple VMs/VNFs in parallel, we will be in trouble.

We need a local variable called untaggedUrl to hold the URL value for each instance.
sendEventAsReqstRequest(final String untaggedUrl, final String event)
Please check your eclipse formatter settings. I can see unwanted spaces in multiple files.
The logic seems to cover all cases now. Good.

suggestion: could do a "Set" operation here, to determine if names contains something that doesn't appear in inputProperty.  Once that's done, could simplify the rest of this by using Stream.reduce(), eliminating the need for checkUrl.  I think that would be clearer, though I could be wrong.
could pre-compile this pattern and stick it into a static
Do we have to parameterize "key" - why doesn't java complain about line 179 since "key" is a generic type, but the method returns a parameterized type?
are the backslashes necessary inside the "[...]" blocks?
could these two be combined - just look for two "{" in a row with no intervening "}"?
I prefer to use assertj, because it's easy to forget things otherwise.  For example, in this case, "fail()" is missing - should appear either after line 215 or after line 217

same comments as for the duplicate code block in the earlier java file

consider extracting this common code block into a utility or super class
consider extracting these common methods into a utility or super class
missing a fail() statement here somewhere - either after line 125 or after line 127
You will only get one entry in the set if there are multiple occurrences of the key in the URL.
replaceAll() is needed for multiple keys.
Trivial comment but maybe set Eclipse to trim lines on file save.
Correct Liam. But, the string replace will replace all the occurences of the key.
replaceAll can take in regular expressions. For simple string substitutions, replace method will do the job by  replacing all the occurrences.
Replace() will replace all the occurrences of the key, so Set is OK.
replace will replace all the key in the Url. test result shows it is OK for the logic.
ack, I will check the code style.
I think this depends on our definition, If we think string like "vnf/id" could be a proper key, then we can change the regex here.
In first case, there is only one { in the Url,
go ahead and compile these to Pattern objects
since these methods are identical to those in the other class, these should be extracted into a utility class that the two can share
Ack, and this will be handled in another ticket POLICY-1884,
Maybe add some test cases to catch syntax errors in url as well.
Ack,we add the syntax error check in *CarrierTechnologyParameters.java, so the syntax error unit test cases are also in *CarrierTechnologyParametersTest.java. I will add some more case there.
